arts.ttl: Barcelona in hot water over illegal lineup
arts.sum: “If a player does not attend or withdraws from the national team due to injury, he may not play for his club for five days after the last match of the international window unless the national federation expressly releases him,” states the FIFA regulation Osasuna is citing in its appeal. As reported by AS and officially confirmed by the club, Osasuna believes that Barcelona fielded an ineligible player due to the presence of Iñigo Martínez on the pitch.

arts.ttl: Barcelona at risk of points deduction after Osasuna launch official appeal
arts.sum: Osasuna have filed a formal complaint against Barcelona's La Liga victory.

arts.ttl: Osasuna claim Barcelona player was ineligible
arts.sum: Osasuna launch an appeal to the Royal Spanish Football Federation, claiming Barcelona defender Inigo Martinez was ineligible to play against them.

arts.ttl: Lamine Yamal Stats: Why the heir to Lionel Messi's Barcelona crown is Europe's best rated player
arts.sum: Lionel Messi left gargantuan boots to fill when he departed Barcelona in 2021. There have been a couple of false dawns as the Blaugrana sought to find the heir to the Argentine’s Camp Nou crown....

arts.ttl: Arsenal handed major transfer boost in pursuit of Barcelona & Man Utd goalkeeper target
arts.sum: Arsenal have received a positive update in their race to sign Espanyol goalkeeper Joan Garcia.
